message:
fix(runtime): detect dropped/cancelled tasks in JoinHandle, scheduler treats fragmentation as non-deadlock

Two runtime fixes:

1. **JoinHandle dropped-task detection (builder.rs)**: When the
   executor side is dropped without producing a result (e.g. the
   runtime shut down and tasks were force-cancelled), the
   JoinHandle now detects this via Arc::strong_count == 1 and
   panics with a clear message instead of hanging indefinitely
   waiting for a result that will never arrive.

2. **Scheduler fragmentation handling (three_lane.rs)**: The
   governor's spectral health integration now treats
   HealthClassification::Fragmented as a topology signal rather
   than a deadlock indicator.  The match arm is updated to use
   the renamed Fragmented variant and the comments clarify that
   mere wait-graph fragmentation is not a trapped wait cycle —
   actual deadlock forcing is owned by the SCC path.

   New test: test_governor_independent_live_tasks_do_not_force_drain_obligations
   verifies that two independent tasks with no dependency edges
   do not trigger obligation drain (which would be a false
   deadlock response).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
